sourceMovie.title: You Get Me
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto You Get Me (2017) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: In Secret
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If You Get Me clicked for you, In Secret (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and romance, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: She's So Lovely
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If You Get Me clicked for you, She's So Lovely (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after You Get Me?
faq.answer: Try In Secret, She's So Lovely, Asylum—each shares DNA with You Get Me while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is You Get Me?
faq.answer: You Get Me (2017) is best described as Thriller, Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is You Get Me worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
